name: Maternity Wellness — Jess
description: Jess's prenatal fitness program starting at week 17. 3 structured workout days (yoga, stretch/mobility, strength) plus walking built into the weekday commute (18 min each way M-F). Workouts are pregnancy-safe but not babied — you'll feel the burn. Weekends are for hiking or rest.
creator: Custom
commute: 18 min walk to work + 18 min walk home (M-F). That's 36 min/day, 180 min/week of easy cardio built in.

weeks.label: Week 17 — Get Moving
weeks.description: Second trimester energy is back. Establish the 3-day workout rhythm alongside your daily commute walks. Sessions are 25-30 min and should feel like real work — not a nap on a mat.

schedule.label: YOGA FLOW
schedule.commute: 18 min walk to work + 18 min walk home
schedule.notes: Jess: 30 min active yoga — warriors, dynamic flows, deep squats. Your legs should feel worked.

schedule.label: COMMUTE WALK
schedule.commute: 18 min walk to work + 18 min walk home
schedule.notes: 36 min of walking from the commute. That's your cardio. Enjoy the rest day.

schedule.label: STRETCH & MOBILITY
schedule.commute: 18 min walk to work + 18 min walk home
schedule.notes: Jess: 25 min active stretch — dynamic warmup, deep hip openers, back relief. Not passive.

activity.name: Weekend Hike
activity.notes: Easy trail, conversational pace. Bring water and snacks. Poles if you have them.

activity.name: Long Walk
activity.notes: Longer weekend walk — explore a new route.

options.label: Yoga
options.notes: Extra yoga session if you're feeling good.

schedule.label: REST
schedule.notes: Full rest. Stretch on the couch if you feel like it.

schedule.label: STRENGTH
schedule.commute: 18 min walk to work + 18 min walk home
schedule.notes: Jess: 30 min strength — 3 rounds of glutes/legs, 3 rounds of upper body, core work. You'll feel it.

schedule.label: COMMUTE WALK
schedule.commute: 18 min walk to work + 18 min walk home
schedule.notes: Commute walk only. Rest from structured work.

tips: Jess: your commute = 36 min of walking/day. That's already great cardio — own it.
Workout days should feel like real work, not just gentle stretching
Warrior holds are meant to burn — hold them strong
NO flat-on-back poses — always side-lying or propped on elbows
Belly compression: don't fold too deep in pigeon or forward folds
Malasana (deep squat) appears in almost every session — it's the best labor prep move
If you feel dizzy, short of breath, or any pain — stop and rest

log.commute: 18 min walk to work + 18 min walk home
log.notes: Very good session. Deep stretches held for way longer than usual. A few positions had too much belly compression — asked for modifications which were good. Some positions were flat on back which caused too much belly pressure — got mods for those too. Took ~38 min including time to read and understand the poses (first time through).
modifications: Belly compression poses needed alternatives — avoid deep forward folds over legs
No flat-on-back poses — use side-lying modifications (happy baby, spinal twists)
Will speed up once poses are familiar — reading time added ~8 min

weeks.label: Week 18 — Push a Little
weeks.description: Same workouts, more confidence. Hold poses longer, add reps where it felt easy, try the harder modification. Your body is adapting to the routine.

schedule.label: YOGA FLOW
schedule.commute: 18 min walk to work + 18 min walk home
schedule.notes: Same flow. Hold warriors 10 seconds longer. Try closing eyes in tree pose.

schedule.label: COMMUTE WALK
schedule.commute: 18 min walk to work + 18 min walk home

schedule.label: STRETCH & MOBILITY
schedule.commute: 18 min walk to work + 18 min walk home
schedule.notes: Same routine. Sink deeper into pigeon (staying upright). Try the side-to-side child's pose variation.

activity.name: Weekend Hike
activity.notes: Slightly longer hike this week if last week felt good.

schedule.label: STRENGTH
schedule.commute: 18 min walk to work + 18 min walk home
schedule.notes: Same workout. Add 2-3 reps to bridges and squats if round 3 felt easy. Try single-leg bridges if you haven't.

schedule.label: COMMUTE WALK
schedule.commute: 18 min walk to work + 18 min walk home

tips: If round 3 of strength didn't burn last week, add reps this week
Single-leg bridges are the upgrade from regular bridges — try them
Your commute walking is building a solid aerobic base without extra effort
Baby is growing — widen your squat stance if belly starts getting in the way
Try a daily malasana hold (even 30 seconds) outside of workouts

weeks.label: Week 19 — Mix It Up
weeks.description: Introduce workout choices. The 30-min mobility flow is a great swap for yoga day. Lower back & hips stretch is available if that area needs attention. Maternity strength flow adds upper body variety.

schedule.label: YOGA / MOBILITY
schedule.commute: 18 min walk to work + 18 min walk home

options.label: 30 Min Mobility Flow
options.notes: Dynamic mobility flow — skip plow pose, modify deep forward folds. Fresh challenge.

schedule.label: COMMUTE WALK
schedule.commute: 18 min walk to work + 18 min walk home

schedule.label: STRETCH & MOBILITY
schedule.commute: 18 min walk to work + 18 min walk home

options.label: Lower Back & Hips
options.notes: 15 min — great if lower back is nagging.

activity.name: Weekend Hike
activity.notes: Trail hike. Bring poles if terrain is uneven — balance changes as belly grows.

options.label: Gentle Yoga
options.notes: 15 min gentle flow — lighter option if the week was tiring.

schedule.label: STRENGTH
schedule.commute: 18 min walk to work + 18 min walk home

options.label: Maternity Strength Flow
options.notes: 25 min — randomized exercises, more upper body and core variety.

schedule.label: COMMUTE WALK
schedule.commute: 18 min walk to work + 18 min walk home

tips: Week 19 — almost halfway! Belly is noticeable now
The mobility flow is more dynamic than the yoga flow — good variety
Lower back pain is common around now — the lower back & hips stretch targets that
Start using poles on hikes if you haven't — your center of gravity is shifting
Choices are good — listen to what your body wants each day

weeks.label: Week 20 — Halfway
weeks.description: Halfway mark. Belly is real and growing. Same 3-day structure with full workout choices. Energy may fluctuate day-to-day but you've built a strong foundation. Push when you can, ease off when you need to.

schedule.label: YOGA / MOBILITY
schedule.commute: 18 min walk to work + 18 min walk home

options.label: 30 Min Mobility Flow
options.notes: Skip plow pose and deep forward folds.

options.label: Gentle Yoga Flow
options.notes: 15 min — lighter option for low-energy days.

schedule.label: COMMUTE WALK
schedule.commute: 18 min walk to work + 18 min walk home

schedule.label: STRETCH & MOBILITY
schedule.commute: 18 min walk to work + 18 min walk home

activity.name: Weekend Hike
activity.notes: Easy trail. May need to slow pace — that's fine.

schedule.label: STRENGTH
schedule.commute: 18 min walk to work + 18 min walk home

options.label: Maternity Gentle Flow
options.notes: Swap to gentle flow if energy is really low — still movement.

schedule.label: COMMUTE WALK
schedule.commute: 18 min walk to work + 18 min walk home

tips: Halfway! You've built 4 weeks of consistent movement — that's huge
Energy fluctuates more now — always have an easier option ready
The belly changes your center of gravity — widen stance, use walls, use poles
Round ligament pain may show up — skip squats those days, do hip stretches instead
Your commute walking alone puts you ahead of most prenatal exercise recommendations
Keep going — consistency matters more than intensity from here on
Jess: 3 workout days (Wed/Fri/Mon) + commute walking (36 min/day M-F) + Saturday hike
Workouts are 25-30 minutes and should feel like real work
The daily commute walk = 180 min/week of cardio built in — that's a solid base
NO flat-on-back — always side-lying or propped on elbows
Belly compression: don't fold too deep in pigeon or forward folds — stay more upright
Hip openers (malasana, pigeon, butterfly) are labor prep — practice them daily
Push yourself where it feels safe, ease off when your body says to
restGuidelines: Workout days: minimal rest between stretches/holds (5-15s), 15-60s between strength sets. No time pressure — take what you need.

overview.duration: Multi-week (starting week 17, new weeks added over time)
overview.goal: Stay strong, flexible, and energized through pregnancy. Build glute and pelvic floor strength for labor. Keep hips open. Maintain cardio through daily walking.
overview.weeklyStructure: Wed: Yoga/Mobility | Thu: Commute Walk | Fri: Stretch/Mobility | Sat: Hike/Active | Sun: Rest | Mon: Strength | Tue: Commute Walk
overview.weeklyCardio: Commute: 18 min walk each way, 5 days/week = 180 min. Saturday hike adds another 45-75 min.
keyPrinciples: The commute walk is your cardio base — 36 min/day, 5 days/week
3 workout days should feel like real work, not just gentle stretching
NO flat-on-back — always side-lying or propped up
Avoid deep forward folds that compress belly — stay more upright
Hip openers and pelvic floor are the priorities for labor prep
Choices expand each week — your body tells you what it needs
